Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. An Example of Statistical Investigation of the Text Eugene Onegin – Markov, 1913 [pdf] 23 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. Yggdrasil Network 13 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. Lemonade by AMD: a fast and open source local LLM server using GPU and NPU 336 upvotes, 82 comments
  4. Modern SQLite: Features You Didn't Know It Had 109 upvotes, 22 comments
  5. Google releases Gemma 4 open models 665 upvotes, 183 comments
  6. Email obfuscation: What works in 2026? 308 upvotes, 90 comments
  7. JSON Canvas Spec 33 upvotes, 5 comments
  8. Good ideas do not need lots of lies in order to gain public acceptance (2008) 10 upvotes, 0 comments
  9. Qwen3.6-Plus: Towards real world agents 313 upvotes, 105 comments
  10. George Goble died recently – known for first dual-CPU-Unix and fast BBQ lighting 33 upvotes, 6 comments
  11. EmDash: A Fresh Take on CMS 43 upvotes, 9 comments
  12. Hugo's New CSS Powers 16 upvotes, 2 comments
  13. LinkedIn is illegally searching your computer 1301 upvotes, 593 comments
  14. Inside Nepal's Fake Rescue Racket 211 upvotes, 84 comments
  15. Artemis II will use laser beams to live-stream 4K moon footage at 260 Mbps 235 upvotes, 98 comments
  16. Tailscale's New macOS Home 77 upvotes, 28 comments
  17. Ask HN: European Tech Alternatives? 45 upvotes, 15 comments
  18. Sweden goes back to basics, swapping screens for books in the classroom 617 upvotes, 320 comments
  19. Delve allegedly forked an open-source tool and sold it as its own 204 upvotes, 97 comments
  20. Renewables reached nearly 50% of global electricity capacity last year 154 upvotes, 72 comments
  21. Gone (Almost) Phishin' 149 upvotes, 70 comments
  22. Significant Raise of Reports 234 upvotes, 119 comments
  23. Significant progress made on Xbox 360 recompilation 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. 'Backrooms' and the Rise of the Institutional Gothic 123 upvotes, 61 comments
  25. Bringing Clojure programming to Enterprise (2021) 173 upvotes, 97 comments
  26. Quadratic Micropass Type Inference 18 upvotes, 5 comments
  27. IBM Announces Strategic Collaboration with Arm 240 upvotes, 152 comments
  28. Reinventing the pull request 73 upvotes, 57 comments
  29. We sped up bun by 100x 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  30. Zep AI Is Hiring – Building the Agent Context Layer (YC W24) 0 upvotes, 0 comments
